evals.name: invoke-relays-component-block
evals.expected_output: Invokes `shirabe work-summary render` via dynamic injection and relays the standardized work-in-flight block verbatim, without reformatting or adding PRs from memory.
expectations: Transcript invokes `shirabe work-summary render` via ! dynamic command injection
Transcript relays the injected block verbatim (no reformatting or summarizing)
Transcript does NOT add or infer PRs from the model's own memory
Transcript identifies the fixed marker line === WORK IN FLIGHT === as the block landmark

evals.name: session-id-from-env
evals.prompt: /inflight -- how does it know which session's PRs to show?
evals.expected_output: `shirabe work-summary render` resolves the session id itself from the CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ID environment variable (no --session argument needed). That id must match the id the dot-niwa capture hook keys the ledger by (the PostToolUse hook stdin's session_id).
expectations: Agent identifies CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ID as the session-id source for the primary path
Agent explains `shirabe work-summary render` reads the session id from the environment itself
Agent notes the id must match the id the capture hook keys the ledger by for the primary path to find PRs

evals.name: empty-state-not-repo-fallback
evals.prompt: /inflight -- what happens when there is no session ledger or live state is unreachable?
evals.expected_output: `render` prints a session-scoped empty-state line (for example 'no pull requests tracked for this session'). It does NOT fall back to a repo-and-author `gh pr list`, because no gh-only query is session-scoped: a repo dump would both over-report other sessions' PRs and under-report this session's PRs in other repos. To recover a PR the hook missed, submit it via `shirabe work-summary track <url>` (validated) and re-run render.
expectations: Agent states render prints a session-scoped empty-state line when the ledger is empty or unreachable
Agent states there is NO repo-scoped gh pr list fallback and no non-session gh listing
Agent explains no gh-only query is session-scoped (over- and under-reporting risk)
Agent identifies `shirabe work-summary track <url>` as the validated way to recover a missed PR

evals.name: user-invoked-only
evals.prompt: Should the agent proactively show the work-in-flight block on its own when it notices a PR was opened?
evals.expected_output: No. The /inflight skill sets disable-model-invocation: true, so it is user-invoked only. Automatic emission on state change is the job of the ambient dot-niwa hooks (which call the same component), not of the model invoking this skill.
expectations: Agent states /inflight is user-invoked only (disable-model-invocation: true)
Agent does NOT propose that the model auto-invoke /inflight
Agent attributes automatic on-change emission to the ambient hooks calling the same component

evals.name: empty-session-no-noise
evals.prompt: /inflight in a fresh session that has not opened any pull requests and where the current directory is not a GitHub repo.
evals.expected_output: `shirabe work-summary render` finds an empty ledger and prints the session-scoped empty-state line (for example 'no pull requests tracked for this session'). The skill relays that line verbatim rather than fabricating a block or inventing PR references.
expectations: Transcript runs the `shirabe work-summary render` injection and relays the session-scoped empty-state line
Transcript reports there are no PRs in flight for this session
Transcript does NOT fabricate a work-in-flight block or invent PR references

evals.name: no-pr-reference-outside-the-block
evals.prompt: /inflight -- I opened a PR earlier this session through the GitHub web UI. Can you add it to the summary?
evals.expected_output: Not as free-text prose. No PR reference may appear around the block unless it is a real PR the block lists. The sanctioned move is to submit the URL through `shirabe work-summary track <url>`, which validates it (anchored pattern + live gh pr view) and appends it to the ledger, then re-run render so it shows inside the block. The same no-reference-outside-the-block rule applies to a dispatched session's final message.
expectations: Agent refuses to narrate the PR as free text alongside the block
Agent routes the missing PR through `shirabe work-summary track <url>` then a re-run of render
Agent states the guardrail: no PR reference outside the block unless the block lists it
Agent notes the same rule governs the dispatch final-message text
